notes: Bespoke implementation of the Support-Polar Zonotope Certificate Network thesis. name: Multi-Order Board Scan Network
short_thesis: A chess board can be read as several short sequences. Different scan orders expose different dependencies: rank-major, file-major, diagonal, spiral-from-king, and center-out. Bespoke CrossStitchCNNTokenFusionNet model: a convolutional board branch and an occupied-piece token branch share the same channel width and are coupled at every stage through per-group 2x2 cross-stitch matrices initialised to the identity; mixed summaries are injected back through learned adapters.

name: Neural Decision Forest BoardNet

Bespoke NeuralDecisionForestBoardNet model: a compact convolutional trunk with mean+max pooling feeds a fully soft differentiable decision forest of oblique soft binary trees with learned leaf logits; the puzzle logit is the mean across trees of each tree's path-probability-weighted leaf logit.

name: Vector-Quantized Motif Codebook Net

Bespoke VectorQuantizedMotifCodebookNet model: a compact convolutional encoder produces per-square motif embeddings that are routed through a learned EMA-updated codebook with a straight-through estimator; the head reads pooled quantized features, the per-batch code-usage histogram, and a learned spatial code-map embedding to produce the puzzle logit.

short_thesis: - Parent family: `Hall-Defect Obligation Matroid Network`
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Credal Temperature Field Network
short_thesis: - Parent family: `Credal Near-Puzzle Evidence Network`
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Sylvester Tactical Coupling Network
short_thesis: Couples a learned attacker operator A and defender operator B through the Sylvester equation A X + X B = C; classifies puzzles from properties of the unique solution X (singular spectrum, resonance gap min |lambda_i(A) + mu_j(B)|).
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Schur-Complement Defender Elimination Network
short_thesis: Block-partitions a learned PSD interaction matrix into attacker/defender squares and classifies from the Schur complement S = D - B^T A^{-1} B; Haynsworth inertia of S exposes residual defensive insolvency.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Bures-Wasserstein SPD Threat Manifold Network
short_thesis: Embeds boards as SPD threat covariances and classifies via Bures-Wasserstein geodesic distances to learned class Frechet means; uses operator geometric mean rather than Fisher-Rao or log-Euclidean geometry.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Numerical-Range Boundary Network
short_thesis: Computes the field-of-values W(A) of a learned non-symmetric chess operator and classifies from boundary-curve descriptors and the non-normality gap numr(A) - rho(A); exposes transient-amplification structure no spectrum-only readout sees.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Lyapunov Stability Threat Network
short_thesis: Treats each board as autonomous dynamics dot x = A x and solves A^T P + P A = -Q for a chess-derived weighting Q; uses inertia, condition number, and trace of P as a quadratic stability certificate distinct from the controllability Gramian.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Pfaffian Skew Threat Network
short_thesis: Builds a skew-symmetric chess operator K and uses its Pfaffian pf(K) (signed perfect-matching enumerator) plus sub-Pfaffian fingerprints; orientation cancellation discriminates puzzle vs near-puzzle at matched ||K||_F.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: p-adic Ultrametric Threat Embedding Network
short_thesis: Embeds squares into Z/p^k Z with a learned p-adic encoder; ultrametric distances and Newton-polygon slopes of the resulting valuation-weighted matrix capture tree-shaped tactical hierarchy.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Free-Probability R-Transform Spectrum Network
short_thesis: Treats attacker A and defender B as freely independent operators and predicts spec(A+B) via free additive convolution (R-transform); the deviation between empirical spectrum and free-conv prediction (free-cumulant mismatch) is a coupling fingerprint.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Williamson Symplectic-Eigenvalue Threat Network
short_thesis: Pairs squares with rule-derived next-action 'momenta', builds a 2n x 2n SPD operator on the resulting phase space, and reads off symplectic eigenvalues from Williamson normal form M = S^T D S (S in Sp(2n, R)); a phase-space invariant outside O(2n).
notes: Research-packet promotion. Bespoke implementation lives in src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/williamson_symplectic_threat_network.py; the idea-local model.py is a thin wrapper around the registered builder.

name: Magnus-BCH Operator-Coupling Series Network
short_thesis: Computes BCH log Z = log(exp(A) exp(B)) as a truncated Magnus series of nested commutators up to weight 4; weight-3 and weight-4 commutator norms capture iterated tactical depth that single-commutator features (i040) cannot detect.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Bespoke implementation lives in src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/magnus_bch_coupling_series_network.py; the idea-local model.py is a thin wrapper around the registered builder.

name: Riccati Optimal-Defense Network
short_thesis: Treats each board as an LQR control problem; solves the algebraic Riccati equation A^T P + P A - P B R^{-1} B^T P + Q = 0 via Schur of the Hamiltonian H = [[A, -BR^{-1}B^T],[-Q,-A^T]]; optimal-defense cost J* = trace(P) and closed-loop spectral margin separate puzzles from non-puzzles.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Bespoke implementation lives in src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/riccati_optimal_defense_network.py; the idea-local model.py is a thin wrapper around the registered builder.

name: Clifford Rotor Threat Network
short_thesis: Embeds squares as multivectors in Cl(3,0) geometric algebra; composes threats via the non-commutative geometric product ab = a.b + a^b; learned rotors R = exp(B/2) act via x -> R x R^{-1}; per-grade pooled features capture rotational threat structure no real-arithmetic packet can express.
notes: Bespoke Clifford-rotor implementation registered as `clifford_rotor_threat_network`; the prior shared `ResearchPacketProbe` scaffold has been removed.

name: Tracy-Widom Level-Spacing Network
short_thesis: Computes eigenvalues of a learned chess Hermitian operator and classifies puzzle-likeness from nearest-neighbor level-spacing statistics: Wigner-Dyson (chaotic, GOE/GUE) versus Poisson (integrable). Mean spacing ratio <r> and spectral form factor are the central invariants.
notes: Research-packet promotion. Scaffold-only ResearchPacketProbe wrapper; not a completed bespoke implementation of the markdown architecture. Do not benchmark or describe this folder as an implemented architecture until bespoke model code replaces the shared probe.

name: Lindstrom-Gessel-Viennot Path Determinant Network
short_thesis: Builds a path-generating-function matrix M[i,j] = sum_paths(s_i -> t_j) prod edge_weights via Neumann series on a learned chess DAG; LGV lemma makes det(M) the signed enumerator of non-intersecting attacker-to-target k-tuples. Distinguishes overload/double-attack motifs algebraically.
notes: Bespoke Lindstrom-Gessel-Viennot path-determinant implementation registered as lindstrom_gessel_viennot_path_network; the prior shared ResearchPacketProbe scaffold has been removed.

name: Toda Isospectral Flow Network
short_thesis: Builds a tridiagonal symmetric chess operator L and evolves it via the Toda Lax flow dot L = [L, B(L)]; isospectral evolution preserves spectrum but sorts diagonal exponentially. Sorting rate, Manakov integrals, and slowest-decaying off-diagonal residues classify puzzle-likeness.
notes: Bespoke implementation of the Toda Lax flow operator network; idea folder wrapper delegates to `chess_nn_playground.models.trunk.toda_isospectral_flow` and the registered `toda_isospectral_flow_network` model.

name: Hadamard Walsh-Spectrum Network
short_thesis: Applies the 64x64 Walsh-Hadamard transform (Sylvester construction) to per-channel pooled square signals; classifies puzzle-likeness from top-k Walsh coefficient energies. Uses the boolean Fourier basis on Z_2^6 -- distinct from DCT, wavelets, and spectral-Laplacian features.

name: Cayley Orthogonal Map Network
short_thesis: Builds skew-symmetric A in R^{r x r} from board features and forms the Cayley map Q = (I-A)(I+A)^{-1} in SO(r); rotates a learned reference basis and uses identity-deviation features. Distinct from polar-Procrustes (i063) and QR -- Cayley is an algebraic identity, not a decomposition.

name: Stable-Rank Multiscale Network
short_thesis: Computes stable rank ||M||_F^2 / ||M||_2^2 of a learned 64x64 chess interaction at three block scales (full, 4 quadrants, 16 sub-blocks). Continuous, differentiable, scale-equivariant; quantifies effective tactical degrees of freedom.

name: Permanent Ryser Coupling Network
short_thesis: Builds top-k attacker x top-k defender bilinear interaction matrix M (k=6); computes its permanent via Ryser's formula -- the unsigned count of perfect attacker-to-defender matchings. Distinct from DPP (signed determinant, i058) and Pfaffian (signed matchings of skew-graphs, i226).

name: Cayley-Hamilton Coefficient Network
short_thesis: Extracts characteristic polynomial coefficients (c_1, ..., c_r) of a learned r x r chess operator via Faddeev-LeVerrier recursion (no eigendecomposition). c_k = (-1)^k e_k(spec) are signed elementary symmetric polynomials of eigenvalues -- combinatorially distinct from raw eigvals and gradient-stable near degenerate spectra.

name: Multi-Stream Chess-Decomposed Transformer Evaluator
target_task: engine evaluation value and policy heads; optional puzzle_binary sanity-check head
short_thesis: Generalizes the i193 exchange/king dual-stream prior into parallel attention streams with an added positional stream for future engine-strength evaluation work.

name: Chess-Decomposed Attention Network
short_thesis: Composes NNUE-style king-conditioned features, i193-style exchange/king decomposition, and LC0-BT4-style square-token attention into a three-stream routed classifier.

name: HalfKA Dual-Stream LC0 Evaluator
target_task: engine-strength chess evaluation with WDL value and LC0 policy heads; optional puzzle_binary sanity-check head
short_thesis: Combines Stockfish NNUE HalfKA accumulators, the i193 exchange/king dual-stream backbone, and LC0-compatible value/policy heads for a future engine-evaluation architecture.

name: Rule-Aware Tactical Head
short_thesis: Additive gated TSDP fusion on the i193 dual-stream trunk; rule-exact terminal-state features (mate_in_1, stalemate_threat, ...) feed a small head that contributes a logit delta gated by the i193 trunk diagnostics.

name: Oriented Tactical Sheaf Laplacian (Fast)
short_thesis: Speed-optimized i018. Same cellular sheaf Laplacian math, same parameters, same numerics, but a GPU-friendlier execution pattern (vectorized chunked relation diffusion plus optional torch.compile) to close i018's wall-clock gap without touching accuracy.
notes: Execution-optimized variant of i018. Bespoke implementation lives in src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/oriented_tactical_sheaf_fast.py; the idea-local model.py is a thin wrapper around the registered builder. Numerical equivalence to i018 is verified (logits ~1e-8, gradients ~1e-10).

name: Learned Relation Confidence Sheaf
target_task: puzzle_binary classification: fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle. Identical contract to i018.
short_thesis: Keep i018's exact side-to-move-oriented chess relation topology, but replace its relation-wide weighting with normalized edge-wise confidence learned from board-only chess features. The model gains the missing degree of freedom (intra-relation edge weighting) without changing i018's incidence builder, diffusion math, or readout.
notes: Implemented as the registered `learned_relation_confidence_sheaf` model in `src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/learned_relation_confidence_sheaf.py`, which subclasses i018's `OrientedTacticalSheafNet` and only adds the new confidence head plus a confidence-weighted forward path. The `flat_confidence: true` ablation exactly reproduces i018 logits with shared weights; the default normalized head reproduces i018 within FP32 reduction noise at zero-init.

name: Candidate Move Forcedness Sheaf
target_task: puzzle_binary classification: fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle. Identical contract to i018.
short_thesis: Wrap i018's static oriented tactical sheaf with a small, deterministic candidate-move bottleneck that explicitly asks whether one move's local structural evidence sharply dominates the alternatives. The trunk emits the i018 base logit and per-square states; a bounded set of pseudo-legal moves is enumerated, scored by source/target sheaf states plus chess flags, and pooled with a top-k softmax. A gated additive delta head augments the base logit so the move branch can only help, not hurt, the i018 baseline.
notes: Implemented as the registered `candidate_move_forcedness_sheaf` model in `src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/candidate_move_forcedness_sheaf.py`, which subclasses i018's `OrientedTacticalSheafNet` and adds a deterministic candidate-move bottleneck plus a gated additive delta head. Zero-init delta+gate exactly reproduces i018 logits on shared weights (max abs diff `0.0` on a 4-sample CPU batch); training is needed before the move branch deviates from the i018 baseline.

name: Pin / X-Ray / Overload Sheaf
target_task: puzzle_binary classification: fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle. Identical contract to i018.
short_thesis: Keep i018's side-to-move-oriented sheaf spine and i249's vectorized diffusion execution path, but replace i018's 12-plane tactical relation graph with a 22-plane graph that adds ten compact dependency planes (x-ray, skewer, discovered-attack candidate, attacks-against-piece-with- pinned-defender, attacks-on-overloaded-defender, each in us/them mirrors). All higher-order motifs are collapsed into pairwise planes or per-square soft masses before diffusion, so the sheaf operator class is unchanged.
notes: Implemented as the registered `pin_xray_overload_sheaf` model in `src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/pin_xray_overload_sheaf.py`. The module ships `TacticalIncidenceBuilderV2` (extends i018's incidence builder), `FastSheafDiffusionBlockV2` (i249 block parameterised by signs and relation count), and `PinXrayOverloadSheafNet`. Falsifier flags `scramble_relations`, `scramble_new_only`, and `family_collapse` are wired in the config so the relation-family ablations from the packet can be run without code edits. Side-specific absolute pins are verified against the packet's pin-bank derivation on synthetic boards.

name: i018 BT4-112 Controlled Encoding
target_task:
puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle,
fine label 2 maps to puzzle). Same input contract as i018.
short_thesis:
Test whether the current repo's BT4-style 112-channel encoding adds usable
board-state signal to the i018 sheaf trunk after the exact 12 mover-oriented
tactical relation masks are kept fixed. The thesis is that any honest BT4
benefit must show up as a small additive lift over the matched simple_18
pathway when the relation scaffold is load-bearing, not as a replacement for
exact chess geometry. Three controlled relation modes (exact / confidence /
hybrid) and four falsifiers isolate the encoding contribution.
notes:
Promoted from ideas/research/packets/classic/i253_i018_bt4_112_controlled_encoding.md.
Bespoke i253 controlled-encoding variant of i018. CRTK metadata is
reporting-only and never used as model input. Default config.yaml is the
exact-relations-only simple18_padded112 control row; switch
data.encoding to lc0_bt4_112 and model.relation_mode to
confidence / hybrid to walk the 36-run base matrix described in the
research markdown. Falsifier knobs (scramble_exact_relations,
augmentation_only) are exposed at config level so each cell is one
config edit.

name: Efficient i018 Scale-XXL
target_task:
puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle,
fine label 2 maps to puzzle). Same contract as i018.
short_thesis:
Honest scale-XXL successor to i018 oriented_tactical_sheaf_laplacian.
Preserve the 12-relation tactical incidence thesis, preserve the current
numerical execution path, and direct the extra scale budget into capacity
that does not multiply irregular relation work: wider square-token
channels and a much larger readout head. An optional grouped low-rank
restriction parameterization is provided behind a config flag for a
later stalk-scaling experiment, but the default first XXL run uses the
i018 full restriction maps so width-only scaling can be evaluated with
no parameterization confound.
notes:
Promoted from ideas/research/packets/classic/i254_efficient_i018_scale_xxl.md.
Bespoke i254 capacity-scaled variant of i018. CRTK metadata is
reporting-only and never used as model input. Default config.yaml is
the recommended first XXL run: width=160, hidden_dim=320, depth=4,
stalk_dim=8, restriction_mode=full, compile_model=false,
fuse_incidence=false.

name: i018 BT4 Distillation Student
target_task:
puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle,
fine label 2 maps to puzzle). Same input contract as i018 and BT4.
short_thesis:
Plain BT4-style residual conv tower that stays dense-convolutional
at inference time but is trained with richer-than-logits supervision
from i018: calibrated teacher logits, six scalar tactical
diagnostics, the typed 12-d relation-density vector, and eight
compact 8x8 relation-summary planes. Mover-oriented canonicalization
is bolted in front of the BT4 stem to inherit i018's cheap symmetry.
The student preserves BT4's CPU-friendly deployment shape and tests
whether explanation-style supervision can recover most of i018's
decision quality at roughly BT4 latency.
source_packet_candidate: Fast Distilled Conv Student for Puzzle Classification
notes:
Promoted from ideas/research/packets/classic/i255_i018_bt4_distillation_student.md.
Bespoke i255 BT4 conv student with mover canonicalization and three
training-time distillation heads (scalar diagnostic, summary plane,
optional readout projector). At inference only `logits` is needed;
the auxiliary heads are tiny additions to the BT4 backbone. CRTK
metadata is reporting-only and never used as model input. Default
`config.yaml` is the recommended `base` student row from the
research markdown (simple_18, channels=64, num_blocks=4); switch
`model.channels=96, model.num_blocks=6` for the `scale_up` row, and
flip `data.encoding=lc0_bt4_112, model.input_channels=112` for the
benchmark-comparability variant. The distillation loss and teacher
target cache are NOT bundled in this packet; the model is trainable
on plain BCE today and the distillation hooks (diagnostic /
summary plane / readout heads) emit predictions that the trainer
picks up automatically once teacher targets are wired into the
loss path.

name: Near Puzzle Rejection Specialist
target_task: puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle).
short_thesis: Reject verified near-puzzles at fixed verified-puzzle recall instead of maximising PR-AUC in isolation. Final logit is raw_claim - softplus(veto), with the veto built from a per-square forcedness-gap field (claim minus reply-escape), a defender-overload margin, a king-escape pressure signal, and a candidate-concentration head over the gap distribution.
notes: Promoted from ideas/research/packets/classic/i256_near_puzzle_rejection_specialist.md. Bespoke implementation lives at src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/near_puzzle_rejection_specialist.py and is registered as `near_puzzle_rejection_specialist`. CRTK metadata is reporting-only and never feeds the forward pass. The candidate set, reply envelope, overload, and king-escape signals are all derived from the simple_18 board through the deterministic DualStreamFeatureBuilder from i193 — no python-chess fallback and no engine search.

name: Promotion Mate Slice Specialist
target_task: puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle).
short_thesis: Improve the three persistently weak benchmark slices (promotion, underpromotion, mate_in_1) by keeping a board-only parent trunk intact and adding four bounded gated logit deltas: a promotion fanout over {Q, R, B, N}, an underpromotion divergence head that scores non-queen vs queen margins, a king-zone forcing-witness head, and a tiny promotion-mate joint overlap branch. The final logit is base + sum_k gate_k * delta_k with Delta-bounded deltas and structural masks that zero each branch when its prerequisites do not hold.
notes: Promoted from ideas/research/packets/classic/i257_promotion_mate_slice_specialist.md. Bespoke implementation lives at src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/promotion_mate_slice_specialist.py and is registered as `promotion_mate_slice_specialist`. CRTK metadata is reporting-only and never feeds the forward pass. The candidate set, promotion fanout, underpromotion margin, mate witness scalars, and joint overlap branch are all derived from the simple_18 board through the deterministic DualStreamFeatureBuilder from i193 — no python-chess fallback and no engine search. The research packet's recommended OrientedTacticalSheafFast (i249) trunk variant is deferred and tracked in implementation_notes.md as a planned follow-up.

name: Relation-Masked Attention Graft over i018
target_task: puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle).
short_thesis: Keep the i018 oriented tactical sheaf trunk intact and add one small post-sheaf sparse attention residual block whose neighborhoods are derived from i018's typed relation masks. The graft selects top-K neighbors per square from the union of the 12 typed relations with a king-zone boost, biases logits with a low-rank relation-conditioned term, and applies the update through a gated residual whose output projection is zero-initialised so the parent trunk is recovered when the graft is silent.
notes: Promoted from ideas/research/packets/classic/i258_relation_masked_attention_i018.md. Bespoke implementation lives at src/chess_nn_playground/models/trunk/relation_masked_attention_i018.py and is registered as `relation_masked_attention_i018`. The graft is designed as a small additive side branch on the i018 trunk rather than a full attention tower; the four neighborhood modes from the packet's ablation grid are exposed as a single config flag. CRTK metadata is reporting-only and never feeds the forward pass.

name: i018 + BT4 Ensemble Compression
target_task: puzzle_binary classification. Fine label 2 maps to puzzle; fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-puzzle (label 1 = near-puzzle, the deployment pain point this idea targets).
short_thesis: Promotes ideas/research/packets/classic/i259_i018_bt4_ensemble_compression.md. Deployment artifact is a BT4-shaped conv student over simple_18; the research-time forward optionally co-evaluates frozen i018 + frozen BT4 teachers so that ensemble logits, disagreement, and entropy can be cached for distillation. Student-only inference matches BT4 latency.
notes: Bespoke ensemble-compression network registered as i018_bt4_ensemble_compression. Source implementation lives in src/chess_nn_playground/models/architecture/i018_bt4_ensemble_compression.py; the idea-local model.py is a thin wrapper around the registered builder. Source research packet remains at ideas/research/packets/classic/i259_i018_bt4_ensemble_compression.md and is referenced by the idea-local math_thesis.md / architecture.md. Teacher checkpoints are loaded at distillation time; the registered builder defaults to teacher_mode=off so the shared trainer and the pytest smoke tests get a clean student-only network.

name: Learned Relation Confidence Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Learned Relation Confidence (LRC). Keep the i018 12-relation tactical
incidence topology fixed and learn a per-edge confidence weight that
only attenuates already-valid edges:

    weighted_mask[r, i, j] = mask[r, i, j] * sigmoid(score[r, i, j])

where ``mask`` comes from the deterministic
``TacticalIncidenceBuilder``. The score combines a low-rank bilinear
on square tokens, a factorised edge MLP over piece descriptors, and a
per-relation gate. Per-(batch, relation) summary tensors (mean
confidence on active edges, total mass, soft kept fraction, entropy)
feed an additive gated delta over the i193 trunk:

    final_logit = base_logit + gate * primitive_delta_raw

Topology preservation is structural: if ``mask[r, i, j] = 0`` then
``weighted_mask[r, i, j] = 0`` exactly. CRTK metadata, source labels,
verification flags, engine evaluations, and principal variations are
not consumed.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_42_learned_relation_confidence_primitive.md.
Reuses the i018 ``TacticalIncidenceBuilder`` for the 12 typed
tactical relations and the i193
``ExchangeThenKingDualStreamNetwork`` trunk for the baseline logit.
The relation builder and board adapter are frozen so the primitive
only learns the confidence layer on top of the deterministic
topology. The source markdown is kept in place at
``ideas/research/primitives/external_42_learned_relation_confidence_primitive.md``
(do not move or rename).

name: Candidate Move Forcedness Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Candidate Move Forcedness (CMF). For every pseudo-legal candidate
move on the side-to-move board, build a deterministic forcedness
descriptor (mover/victim value, capture/check/promotion seeds,
source/target mobility, move-class flags, SEE-lite gain) plus a
light per-edge scorer over the rule-derived (B, 64, 64) legal-move
graph, then top-k pool the scores into a board-level feature vector
that augments the i193 trunk via an additive gated logit delta:

    final_logit = base_logit + gate * primitive_delta_raw

The legal-move graph and forcedness descriptors are stop-gradient
rule-derived tensors. CRTK metadata, source labels, verification
flags, engine evaluations, and principal variations are not consumed.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_43_candidate_move_forcedness_primitive.md.
Reuses the existing `compute_legal_move_graph` helper from
`src/chess_nn_playground/models/primitives/legal_move_graph.py` for
the (B, 64, 64) pseudo-legal adjacency with move-type and
ray-direction codes, and the i193
`ExchangeThenKingDualStreamNetwork` trunk for the baseline logit.
The legal-move graph and the deterministic forcedness descriptors
are stop-gradient. The source markdown is kept in place at
`ideas/research/primitives/external_43_candidate_move_forcedness_primitive.md`
(do not move or rename). Expensive post-move board simulation (the
source primitive's full `apply_moves_batched`) is intentionally
deferred behind the dense pilot.

name: Pin / X-ray / Skewer Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Tensorised ordered-blocker primitive (PXS). Along each of the 8
queen-style ray directions, identify the first and second occupied
squares from every source square via ``cumsum`` over the existing
``ray_geometry`` table. From those, read off six typed event masses
per source square: one-blocker x-ray pressure, absolute pin, relative
pin, discovered-attack candidate, skewer (front-target value greater
than back-target value), and a pinned-defender proxy that weights the
pin event by the value of the pinned piece itself. Direction-sum to
per-square channels, pool to a 12-dim summary, fuse with the i193
trunk joint pool feature, and project to a scalar additive gated
logit delta. The primitive runs entirely in fused tensor ops -- no
Python loops over rays, sources, or step lengths at train time.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_44_pin_xray_skewer_primitive.md.
The research markdown uses the internal name ``p043``; the registry
slot was reassigned to ``p049`` because ``p043`` was already taken
by ``grassmann_rook_pool``. Phase-1 of the spec ("standalone head +
i018 hybrid") is what is implemented here, but the i193 trunk plays
the role of the strong baseline. Phase-3 native i018 relation
integration is deferred.

name: Defender Overload Triad Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Square-centric defender-overload primitive (DOT). Per side
σ ∈ {us, them} compute attacker / defender masks
``Aσ, Dσ ∈ {0,1}^(B×N×N)`` from the rule-derived attack tables,
restrict to enemy-occupied targets, weight each target by a small
criticality MLP cσ(t) over (a, d, p, a_val, d_val, m_att, m_def,
v_tar), and exploit the identity
``L_d^2 - Σ_t O_dt^2 = Σ_{t≠u} O_dt O_du`` to read off the
*defender-identity reuse* mass without ever materialising the
attacker × target × defender triple. Pins are first-class via a
cumsum-based pin detector on the i193 ray geometry. The 20-dim
operator vector ``[S_us, S_them, S_us-S_them, |S_us-S_them|]`` is
fused with the i193 trunk joint pool, gated with a sigmoid head
(initial bias -2.0 so the i193 baseline is recovered at training
start), and added as a logit delta. Runs in ``O(BN^2)`` per side.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_45_defender_overload_triad_primitive.md.
The research markdown uses the internal name ``p044``; the registry
slot was reassigned to ``p050`` because ``p044`` was already taken
by ``weighted_hodge_projector``. A later promotion queue requested
the slot ``p054`` for the same source markdown; that slot is
intentionally not used because ``p050`` is already the canonical
promotion of this primitive (model + config + idea-local wrapper +
registry manifest entry + focused tests). Treat ``p054`` as a
withdrawn alias of ``p050`` for this source markdown. Phase-1 of the
spec ("standalone overload head, additive on a strong baseline") is
what is implemented here, but the i193 trunk plays the role of the
strong baseline rather than i018. Phase-3 native i018 relation
integration is deferred.

name: King-Zone Reply Pressure Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Board-local, side-to-move-asymmetric king-pressure primitive (KZRP).
Per side σ ∈ {us, them} compute weighted attacker / defender masks
on the defender's king ring and three forward-rank squares, the
pin-discounted "free" defender mass, three escape-class counts
(live, sealed, blocked), the king-square attack mass (a checker
proxy), and a log-bounded reply-capacity proxy. The 8-feature side
vector is concatenated to a 32-dim operator
``[S_us, S_them, S_us - S_them, |S_us - S_them|]``, appended to the
i193 trunk joint pool, gated by a sigmoid head (initial bias -2.0 so
the i193 baseline is recovered at training start) and added as a
logit delta. Pin detection reuses the cumsum-based detector shared
with p049 / p050. Forward path is pure tensor algebra; no Python
loops at training time except the ``O(8)`` per-direction gather in
the pin detector.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_46_king_zone_reply_pressure_primitive.md.
The research markdown uses the internal name ``p045``; the registry
slot was reassigned to ``p051`` because ``p045`` was already taken
by ``kirchhoff_mobility_solve``. Phase-1 of the spec ("standalone
KZRP head, additive on a strong baseline") is what is implemented
here, but the i193 trunk plays the role of the strong baseline
rather than i018. Phase-2 (raw KZRP vector terms appended to i018
``TacticalReadout``) and phase-3 (BT4 plane augmentation /
mixer-native variant) are deferred.

name: Promotion and Underpromotion Geometry Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Promotion and Underpromotion Geometry Primitive (PUGP). Build a fixed
board-only feature vector summarising side-to-move-canonicalised
promotion geometry: per-row own/opp pawn-distance summaries, per-file
candidate masks for quiet and capture promotions on canonical row 0,
and per-arrival-square per-promotion-type (Q, R, B, N) features --
gives-check, enemy-king-zone overlap, sliding-and-knight attacker /
defender counts on the arrival square (safety score), and the
knight-fork hint over enemy high-value targets. Underpromotion is
encoded as the explicit delta-to-queen difference per non-queen type
plus a dedicated knight-fork channel. The pooled vector feeds an
additive gated logit-delta head on top of the i193
ExchangeThenKingDualStreamNetwork trunk.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_47_promotion_underpromotion_primitive.md
(the "PUGP" primitive recommended there as a board-only,
side-to-move-canonicalised, rule-exact promotion-geometry primitive
complementary to i246 PFCT). The source markdown also recommends
rule-exact precompute into Parquet columns as the production-time
feature path; that precompute script is left as a follow-up. The
current model computes the rule-derived feature vector inline on
the simple_18 tensor inside forward without any python-chess call,
which keeps the primitive board-only and matches the same `simple_18`
input the i193 trunk receives.

name: Legal-Move-Graph Pressure-Delta Primitive
target_task:
puzzle_binary classification (fine labels 0 and 1 map to non-
puzzle, fine label 2 maps to puzzle).
short_thesis:
p053 (LMGDP) is the **board-aware pressure-delta** primitive
proposed in
``ideas/research/primitives/external_48_legal_move_graph_delta_primitive.md``.
It reuses the same per-piece-type typed legal-move adjacency p009
(LMGConv) compiles -- so both primitives share a single
rule-exact topology source -- but adds a small bank of explicit
per-edge **pressure-delta** scalar features that p009's
SAGE-style per-type linear aggregation cannot express
(``is_capture``, ``into_king_zone``, ``gives_check_proxy``,
``enemy_value_at_target``, ``pre_opp_attackers_at_target``,
``pre_own_defenders_at_target``,
``mover_post_attack_value_from_t``,
``mover_post_defender_value_from_t``). Per-edge maps are masked
by the typed adjacency, aggregated per arrival square and per
piece type, and projected through a small MLP to a gated
additive logit-delta on top of the i193
ExchangeThenKingDualStreamNetwork trunk.
notes:
Promoted from
``ideas/research/primitives/external_48_legal_move_graph_delta_primitive.md``
(the source markdown proposes a board-aware candidate-move
graph head with pressure-delta features as the load-bearing
signal). The source's preferred slug ``legal_move_graph_delta``
is already taken by the existing p009 LMGConv implementation
(``ideas/registry/p009_legal_move_graph_delta``), and renaming
p009 would break that primitive's existing tests, INDEX entry,
and report links. To keep both implementations coexisting and
avoid touching legacy files the new primitive uses the
disambiguated slug ``legal_move_graph_delta_pressure`` and the
matching folder ``p053_legal_move_graph_delta_pressure``; the
``_pressure`` suffix reflects the load-bearing **pressure-
delta** edge features that distinguish this primitive from
p009's pure LMGConv routing. The source primitive markdown is
unchanged and is referenced from the architecture and math
thesis docs.

name: Efficient Ray Occlusion Scan
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Compact tensorized ray-occlusion scan that operates over the legal
queen-direction ray representation (``RayGeometry``: 8 dirs * 64
squares * 7 steps = 3584 padded slots / 1456 valid cells) rather than
the dense ``(B, 64, 64, 64)`` source-target-between cube. The forward
path is a single ``gather + cumsum + equality + masked reduction`` -
no Python loop over directions or steps. The compact tensor preserves
first-blocker and second-blocker identity / value, so x-rays, pin
candidates, and discovered-attack candidates fall out as cheap
reductions on the same scan output.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_49_efficient_ray_occlusion_scan_primitive.md
(single-proposal markdown). The research markdown originally used
the placeholder slug ``p048_efficient_ray_occlusion_scan_primitive``;
the queue assigns ``p054`` (next free primitive slot after p046) and
the slug ``efficient_ray_occlusion_scan`` (without the ``_primitive``
suffix used in source filenames). The dense-edge scatter mode
described in the research markdown (``rook_visible`` / ``bishop_visible``
/ ``rook_xray`` / ``bishop_xray`` per ``(B, 64, 64)``) is deferred
behind a future i018 graph-builder integration; the implemented
primitive operates in the compact direction-major layout that drives
the additive logit delta head.

name: Near-Puzzle Hard-Negative Veto Primitive
target_task: puzzle_binary classification on the chess-nn-playground splits.
short_thesis:
Near-Puzzle Hard-Negative Veto. Operationalize "verified force minus
safe-reply mass" as a compact board-only diagnostic vector

    z(x) = [FG*, FG(m*), Disc(m*), Conc, Gap12, Avail, RCI,
            dBal, KEP, DOA, Counter]

computed from learned candidate/reply token pools over the i193
ExchangeThenKingDualStreamNetwork trunk's spatial features. The veto
head emits a softplus pressure ``veto(x)`` and contributes a
*negative* additive logit delta gated by a learned sigmoid, so high
veto pressure lowers the puzzle logit on near-puzzle-like positions.
notes:
Promoted from
ideas/research/primitives/external_50_near_puzzle_hard_negative_primitive.md.
The forward path realizes a *board-only* proxy of the math thesis:
candidate/reply enumeration is replaced by learned query-token
attention so the head is differentiable, GPU-friendly, and consumes
no metadata. python-chess move generation is deferred to a possible
future "verified candidate compiler" upgrade once the diagnostic
direction is validated. Mining of near-puzzle hard negatives at the
sampler level is intentionally out of scope for this scaffold;
it would land in the trainer, not the primitive itself.
